* *[Live](/wiki/Live_(Weir/Wasserman_album) "Live (Weir/Wasserman album)")*

(April 1, 1952 – June 29, 2016)[[1]](#cite_note-NYT-1) was an American composer and bass player. A Grammy Award and NEA grant winner, he played and recorded with a wide variety of musicians including [Bob Weir](/wiki/Bob_Weir "Bob Weir"), [Bruce Cockburn](/wiki/Bruce_Cockburn "Bruce Cockburn"), [Elvis Costello](/wiki/Elvis_Costello "Elvis Costello"), [Ani di Franco](/wiki/Ani_di_Franco "Ani di Franco"), [Jerry Garcia](/wiki/Jerry_Garcia "Jerry Garcia"), [David Grisman](/wiki/David_Grisman "David Grisman"), [Stéphane Grappelli](/wiki/St%C3%A9phane_Grappelli "Stéphane Grappelli"), [Rickie Lee Jones](/wiki/Rickie_Lee_Jones "Rickie Lee Jones"), [Van Morrison](/wiki/Van_Morrison "Van Morrison"), [Steve Morse](/wiki/Steve_Morse "Steve Morse"), [Aaron Neville](/wiki/Aaron_Neville "Aaron Neville"), [Lou Reed](/wiki/Lou_Reed "Lou Reed"), [Pete Seeger](/wiki/Pete_Seeger "Pete Seeger"), [Jules Shear](/wiki/Jules_Shear "Jules Shear"), [Brian Wilson](/wiki/Brian_Wilson "Brian Wilson"), [Chris Whitley](/wiki/Chris_Whitley "Chris Whitley"), [Neil Young](/wiki/Neil_Young "Neil Young"), [Jackson Browne](/wiki/Jackson_Browne "Jackson Browne"), [Laurie Anderson](/wiki/Laurie_Anderson "Laurie Anderson"), [Stephen Perkins](/wiki/Stephen_Perkins "Stephen Perkins"), [Banyan](/wiki/Banyan_(band) "Banyan (band)"), [Mystic Knights of the Oingo Boingo](/wiki/Mystic_Knights_of_the_Oingo_Boingo "Mystic Knights of the Oingo Boingo"), and [Ratdog](/wiki/Ratdog "Ratdog").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* *[Quintet '80](/wiki/Quintet_%2780 "Quintet '80")* 1980
* *[New York](/wiki/New_York_(album) "New York (album)")* (1989)
* *[Flying Cowboys](/wiki/Flying_Cowboys "Flying Cowboys")* (1989)
* *[Beautiful Vision](/wiki/Beautiful_Vision "Beautiful Vision")*, [Van Morrison](/wiki/Van_Morrison "Van Morrison") (1982)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American composer and bassist

| Rob Wasserman | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1952-04-01)April 1, 1952  [San Mateo](/wiki/San_Mateo,_California "San Mateo, California"), California, U.S. |
| Died | June 29, 2016(2016-06-29) (aged 64)  Los Angeles |
| Genres | [Rock](/wiki/Rock_music "Rock music"), [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz"), [folk](/wiki/American_folk_music "American folk music") |
| Occupation | Musician |
| Instrument | Double bass |

Musical artist

**Rob Wasserman** (April 1, 1952 – June 29, 2016)[[1]](#cite_note-NYT-1) was an American composer and bass player. A Grammy Award and NEA grant winner, he played and recorded with a wide variety of musicians including [Bob Weir](/wiki/Bob_Weir "Bob Weir"), [Bruce Cockburn](/wiki/Bruce_Cockburn "Bruce Cockburn"), [Elvis Costello](/wiki/Elvis_Costello "Elvis Costello"), [Ani di Franco](/wiki/Ani_di_Franco "Ani di Franco"), [Jerry Garcia](/wiki/Jerry_Garcia "Jerry Garcia"), [David Grisman](/wiki/David_Grisman "David Grisman"), [Stéphane Grappelli](/wiki/St%C3%A9phane_Grappelli "Stéphane Grappelli"), [Rickie Lee Jones](/wiki/Rickie_Lee_Jones "Rickie Lee Jones"), [Van Morrison](/wiki/Van_Morrison "Van Morrison"), [Steve Morse](/wiki/Steve_Morse "Steve Morse"), [Aaron Neville](/wiki/Aaron_Neville "Aaron Neville"), [Lou Reed](/wiki/Lou_Reed "Lou Reed"), [Pete Seeger](/wiki/Pete_Seeger "Pete Seeger"), [Jules Shear](/wiki/Jules_Shear "Jules Shear"), [Brian Wilson](/wiki/Brian_Wilson "Brian Wilson"), [Chris Whitley](/wiki/Chris_Whitley "Chris Whitley"), [Neil Young](/wiki/Neil_Young "Neil Young"), [Jackson Browne](/wiki/Jackson_Browne "Jackson Browne"), [Laurie Anderson](/wiki/Laurie_Anderson "Laurie Anderson"), [Stephen Perkins](/wiki/Stephen_Perkins "Stephen Perkins"), [Banyan](/wiki/Banyan_(band) "Banyan (band)"), [Mystic Knights of the Oingo Boingo](/wiki/Mystic_Knights_of_the_Oingo_Boingo "Mystic Knights of the Oingo Boingo"), and [Ratdog](/wiki/Ratdog "Ratdog").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

He is best known for his own work on the trilogy of albums, *Solo*, *Duets*, and *Trios*.[[5]](#cite_note-AMG-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rob_Wasserman&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

Wasserman started playing violin, and graduated to the bass after his teenage years. He studied at the [San Francisco Conservatory of Music](/wiki/San_Francisco_Conservatory_of_Music "San Francisco Conservatory of Music") where he studied composing with [John Adams](/wiki/John_Adams_(composer) "John Adams (composer)") and double bass with San Francisco Symphony bassists.[[5]](#cite_note-AMG-5)

He worked with [Van Morrison](/wiki/Van_Morrison "Van Morrison"), [Oingo Boingo](/wiki/Oingo_Boingo "Oingo Boingo"), and [David Grisman](/wiki/David_Grisman "David Grisman"). His 1983 album *Solo* won *[Down Beat](/wiki/Down_Beat "Down Beat")* magazine's Record of the Year award. On the albums *Duets* and *Trios*, he worked with [Bobby McFerrin](/wiki/Bobby_McFerrin "Bobby McFerrin"), [Rickie Lee Jones](/wiki/Rickie_Lee_Jones "Rickie Lee Jones"), [Cheryl Bentyne](/wiki/Cheryl_Bentyne "Cheryl Bentyne"), [Lou Reed](/wiki/Lou_Reed "Lou Reed"), [Stéphane Grappelli](/wiki/St%C3%A9phane_Grappelli "Stéphane Grappelli"), [Jerry Garcia](/wiki/Jerry_Garcia "Jerry Garcia"), [Brian Wilson](/wiki/Brian_Wilson "Brian Wilson"), [Willie Dixon](/wiki/Willie_Dixon "Willie Dixon"), [Branford Marsalis](/wiki/Branford_Marsalis "Branford Marsalis"), [Bob Weir](/wiki/Bob_Weir "Bob Weir"), [Edie Brickell](/wiki/Edie_Brickell "Edie Brickell"), [Les Claypool](/wiki/Les_Claypool "Les Claypool"), [Neil Young](/wiki/Neil_Young "Neil Young"), and [Elvis Costello](/wiki/Elvis_Costello "Elvis Costello").

*Duets* was nominated for three Grammy Awards. Bobby McFerrin won for "Brothers", which was performed with Wasserman. Wasserman also won Holland's Edison Award for Record of the Year.

His 2000 album, *Space Island*, incorporated more contemporary musical elements. [RatDog](/wiki/RatDog "RatDog"), which he co-founded with Bob Weir from the Grateful Dead, occupied much of his time. He toured extensively with Lou Reed.

Wasserman was a judge for the 6th–10th annual Independent Music Awards.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Wasserman died of cancer on June 29, 2016, at age 64.[[9]](#cite_note-9) He was remembered by KQED as "the [Grammy-winning](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards") bassist whose playing accompanied everyone from Van Morrison to Willie Dixon."[[10]](#cite_note-10) He was interred in Salem Memorial Park and Garden in [Colma, California](/wiki/Colma,_California "Colma, California").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rob_Wasserman&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Discography")]

### As leader

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rob_Wasserman&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: As leader")]

* *Solo* ([Rounder](/wiki/Rounder_Records "Rounder Records"), 1983)
* *Duets* ([MCA](/wiki/MCA_Records "MCA Records"), 1988)
* *Trios* ([GRP](/wiki/GRP_Records "GRP Records"), 1994)
* *Space Island* ([Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Records "Atlantic Records"), 2000)
* *[Cosmic Farm](/wiki/Cosmic_Farm "Cosmic Farm")* ([Tone Center](/wiki/Tone_Center_Records "Tone Center Records"), 2005)

### As co-leader

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rob_Wasserman&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: As co-leader")]

* *[Live](/wiki/Live_(Weir/Wasserman_album) "Live (Weir/Wasserman album)")*, with [Bob Weir](/wiki/Bob_Weir "Bob Weir") (1999)
* *[Fall 1989: The Long Island Sound](/wiki/Fall_1989:_The_Long_Island_Sound "Fall 1989: The Long Island Sound")*, with Bob Weir and [Jerry Garcia Band](/wiki/Jerry_Garcia_Band "Jerry Garcia Band") (2013)
* *Dua*, with [Sultan Khan](/wiki/Sultan_Khan_(musician) "Sultan Khan (musician)") (2001)

### As sideman

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rob_Wasserman&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: As sideman")]

**With [David Grisman](/wiki/David_Grisman "David Grisman")**

* *[Quintet '80](/wiki/Quintet_%2780 "Quintet '80")* 1980
* *[Stephane Grappelli/David Grisman Live](/wiki/Stephane_Grappelli/David_Grisman_Live "Stephane Grappelli/David Grisman Live")* with [Stéphane Grappelli](/wiki/St%C3%A9phane_Grappelli "Stéphane Grappelli") (1981)
* *[Mondo Mando](/wiki/Mondo_Mando "Mondo Mando")* (1981)

**With [Lou Reed](/wiki/Lou_Reed "Lou Reed")**

* *[New York](/wiki/New_York_(album) "New York (album)")* (1989)
* *[Magic and Loss](/wiki/Magic_and_Loss "Magic and Loss")* (1992)
* *[Lulu](/wiki/Lulu_(Lou_Reed_and_Metallica_album) "Lulu (Lou Reed and Metallica album)")* with [Metallica](/wiki/Metallica "Metallica") (2011)

**With [Rickie Lee Jones](/wiki/Rickie_Lee_Jones "Rickie Lee Jones")**

* *[Flying Cowboys](/wiki/Flying_Cowboys "Flying Cowboys")* (1989)
* *[Naked Songs – Live and Acoustic](/wiki/Naked_Songs_%E2%80%93_Live_and_Acoustic "Naked Songs – Live and Acoustic")* (1995)

**With others**

* *[Beautiful Vision](/wiki/Beautiful_Vision "Beautiful Vision")*, [Van Morrison](/wiki/Van_Morrison "Van Morrison") (1982)
* *[Mighty Like a Rose](/wiki/Mighty_Like_a_Rose "Mighty Like a Rose")*, [Elvis Costello](/wiki/Elvis_Costello "Elvis Costello") (1991)
* *The Original Wang Dang Doodle*, [Willie Dixon](/wiki/Willie_Dixon "Willie Dixon") (1995)
* *The Charity of Night*, [Bruce Cockburn](/wiki/Bruce_Cockburn "Bruce Cockburn") (1996)
* *[Evening Moods](/wiki/Evening_Moods "Evening Moods")*, [RatDog](/wiki/RatDog "RatDog") (2000)
* *[Live at Roseland](/wiki/Live_at_Roseland "Live at Roseland")*, RatDog (2001)
* *Summertime*, Nineteen Thirteen (2015)[[12]](#cite_note-12)
